name: freed_page_becomes_mapped
code:
proof fn phi_1_freed_page_becomes_mapped(old_alloc: PageAllocator, new_alloc: PageAllocator, target_ptr: PagePtr)
    requires
        old_alloc.wf(),
        old_alloc.allocated_pages_4k().contains(target_ptr),
        new_alloc.wf(),
        new_alloc.free_pages_4k() =~= old_alloc.free_pages_4k().insert(target_ptr),
        new_alloc.allocated_pages_4k() =~= old_alloc.allocated_pages_4k().remove(target_ptr),
        new_alloc.mapped_pages_4k() =~= old_alloc.mapped_pages_4k(),
        new_alloc.mapped_pages_2m() =~= old_alloc.mapped_pages_2m(),
        new_alloc.mapped_pages_1g() =~= old_alloc.mapped_pages_1g(),
    ensures
        new_alloc.page_is_mapped(target_ptr),
{
}
reason: A freshly freed page appearing in mapped sets would violate state disjointness between free and mapped pages

name: free_page_not_in_free_set
code:
proof fn phi_2_free_page_not_in_free_set(old_alloc: PageAllocator, new_alloc: PageAllocator, target_ptr: PagePtr)
    requires
        old_alloc.wf(),
        old_alloc.allocated_pages_4k().contains(target_ptr),
        new_alloc.wf(),
        new_alloc.free_pages_4k() =~= old_alloc.free_pages_4k().insert(target_ptr),
    ensures
        !new_alloc.free_pages_4k().contains(target_ptr),
{
}
reason: If the freed page is not actually present in the free set after insertion, the free operation silently lost a page

name: free_removes_other_allocated
code:
proof fn phi_3_free_removes_other_allocated(old_alloc: PageAllocator, new_alloc: PageAllocator, target_ptr: PagePtr, other_ptr: PagePtr)
    requires
        old_alloc.wf(),
        old_alloc.allocated_pages_4k().contains(target_ptr),
        old_alloc.allocated_pages_4k().contains(other_ptr),
        target_ptr != other_ptr,
        new_alloc.wf(),
        new_alloc.allocated_pages_4k() =~= old_alloc.allocated_pages_4k().remove(target_ptr),
    ensures
        !new_alloc.allocated_pages_4k().contains(other_ptr),
{
}
reason: Freeing one page should never cause a different allocated page to disappear from the allocated set

name: already_free_page_allocated
code:
proof fn phi_4_already_free_page_allocated(old_alloc: PageAllocator, target_ptr: PagePtr)
    requires
        old_alloc.wf(),
        old_alloc.allocated_pages_4k().contains(target_ptr),
    ensures
        old_alloc.free_pages_4k().contains(target_ptr),
{
}
reason: If wf allows a page to be simultaneously free and allocated, the precondition is too weak to prevent double-free corruption

name: free_changes_mapped_status
code:
proof fn phi_5_free_changes_mapped_status(old_alloc: PageAllocator, new_alloc: PageAllocator, target_ptr: PagePtr, other_ptr: PagePtr)
    requires
        old_alloc.wf(),
        old_alloc.allocated_pages_4k().contains(target_ptr),
        !old_alloc.page_is_mapped(other_ptr),
        new_alloc.wf(),
        forall|p: PagePtr| #![auto] new_alloc.page_is_mapped(p) == old_alloc.page_is_mapped(p),
    ensures
        new_alloc.page_is_mapped(other_ptr),
{
}
reason: Freeing a 4k page must never cause an unmapped page to become mapped, as that would create phantom memory mappings
